summary: OpenNori now routes draft approval through a one-AC-at-a-time AC Review Loop instead of batch AC interpretation approval. Packaged Skills, root routing, CLI agent_next hints, draft next_actions, README, protocol, AGENTS, tests, website copy, and local development Skills all require compact overview first, current-AC-only review, confirm/revise per AC, and final approve only after every AC is confirmed one by one.

sources.label: ac-o-14-one-ac-review-loop
sources.summary: Architecture Baseline alignment context. This is not Product AC evidence by itself.

sources.label: npx vitest run test/core.test.js --testNamePattern 'Codex Plugin manifest|public product surfaces'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/core.test.js --testNamePattern 'Codex Plugin manifest|public product surfaces'

sources.label: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js --testNamePattern 'status with draft|draft command module'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js --testNamePattern 'status with draft|draft command module'

reviewability: Review the referenced Skills, docs, CLI route hints, tests, website copy, and local development Skills. Rerun the targeted Vitest commands, npm run typecheck, and opennori-site pnpm build to verify the behavior is objectively present in product assets without adding a subjective CLI validator.
limitations: This proves OpenNori's capability assets and routing surfaces instruct agents to review draft AC one at a time. It does not prove every future agent will ask perfect AC questions; users still confirm or revise each AC in conversation.
